1: module Search
2:   class Article
3:     DEFAULT_SORT_BY = "hotness_score DESC, comments_count DESC".freeze
4: 
5:     def self.search_documents(
6:       term: nil,
7:       user_id: nil,
8:       sort_by: nil,
9:       sort_direction: nil,
10:       page: nil,
11:       per_page: nil
12:     )
13:       relation = Homepage::ArticlesQuery.call(user_id: user_id, page: page, per_page: per_page)
14: 
15:       relation = relation.search_articles(term) if term.present?
16: 
17:       relation = sort(relation, term, sort_by, sort_direction)
18: 
19:       Homepage::ArticleSerializer.serialized_collection_from(relation: relation)
20:     end
21: 
22:     def self.sort(relation, term, sort_by, sort_direction)
23:       # By skipping ordering, we rely on the custom ranking defined in the article's tsvector document
24:       return relation if term.present? && sort_by.blank?
25: 
26:       return relation.reorder(sort_by => sort_direction) if sort_by&.to_sym == :published_at && sort_direction
27: 
28:       relation.reorder(DEFAULT_SORT_BY)
29:     end
30:     private_class_method :sort
31:   end
32: end

1: module Homepage
2:   class ArticlesQuery
3:     ATTRIBUTES = %i[
4:       cached_tag_list
5:       comments_count
6:       crossposted_at
7:       displayed_comments_count
8:       id
9:       organization_id
10:       path
11:       public_reactions_count
12:       published_at
13:       reading_time
14:       title
15:       type_of
16:       user_id
17:       video_duration_in_seconds
18:       video_thumbnail_url
19:     ].freeze
20:     DEFAULT_PER_PAGE = 60
21:     MAX_PER_PAGE = 100
22: 
23:     SORT_PARAMS = %i[hotness_score public_reactions_count published_at].freeze
24:     DEFAULT_SORT_DIRECTION = :desc
25: 
26:     def self.call(...)
27:       new(...).call
28:     end
29: 
30:     # TODO: [@rhymes] change frontend to start from page 1
31:     def initialize(
32:       approved: nil,
33:       published_at: nil,
34:       user_id: nil,
35:       organization_id: nil,
36:       tags: [],
37:       hidden_tags: [],
38:       sort_by: nil,
39:       sort_direction: nil,
40:       page: 0,
41:       per_page: DEFAULT_PER_PAGE
42:     )
43:       @relation = Article.published.from_subforem.select(*ATTRIBUTES)
44:         .includes(:distinct_reaction_categories)
45: 
46:       @approved = approved
47:       @published_at = published_at
48:       @user_id = user_id
49:       @organization_id = organization_id
50:       @tags = tags.presence || []
51:       @hidden_tags = hidden_tags.presence || []
52: 
53:       @sort_by = sort_by
54:       @sort_direction = sort_direction || DEFAULT_SORT_DIRECTION
55: 
56:       @page = page.to_i + 1
57:       @per_page = [(per_page || DEFAULT_PER_PAGE).to_i, MAX_PER_PAGE].min
58:     end
59: 
60:     def call
61:       filter.merge(sort).merge(paginate)
62:     end
63: 
64:     private
65: 
66:     attr_reader :relation, :approved, :published_at, :user_id, :organization_id, :tags, :hidden_tags,
67:                 :sort_by, :sort_direction, :page, :per_page
68: 
69:     def filter
70:       @relation = @relation.full_posts
71:       @relation = @relation.where(approved: approved) unless approved.nil?
72:       @relation = @relation.where(published_at: published_at) if published_at.present?
73:       @relation = @relation.where(user_id: user_id) if user_id.present?
74:       @relation = @relation.where(organization_id: organization_id) if organization_id.present?
75:       @relation = @relation.cached_tagged_with_any(tags) if tags.any?
76:       @relation = @relation.not_cached_tagged_with_any(hidden_tags) if hidden_tags.any?
77:       @relation = @relation.includes(:distinct_reaction_categories)
78:       @relation = @relation.where("score >= 0") # Never return negative score articles
79:       @relation = @relation.from_subforem
80: 
81:       relation
82:     end
83: 
84:     def sort
85:       return relation unless SORT_PARAMS.include?(sort_by&.to_sym)
86: 
87:       relation.order(sort_by => sort_direction)
88:     end
89: 
90:     def paginate
91:       relation.page(page).per(per_page)
92:     end
93:   end
94: end

1: module Homepage
2:   class ArticleSerializer < ApplicationSerializer
3:     # @param relation [ActiveRecord::Relation<Article>]
4:     #
5:     # @return [Hash]
6:     def self.serialized_collection_from(relation:)
7:       # Unfortunately the FlareTag class sends one SQL query per each article,
8:       # as we want to optimize by loading them in one query, we're using a different class
9:       tag_flares = Homepage::FetchTagFlares.call(relation)
10: 
11:       # including user and organization as the last step as they are not needed
12:       # by the query that fetches tag flares, they are only needed by the serializer
13:       relation = relation.includes(:user, :organization)
14: 
15:       new(relation, params: { tag_flares: tag_flares }, is_collection: true)
16:         .serializable_hash[:data]
17:         .pluck(:attributes)
18:     end
19: 
20:     attributes(
21:       :class_name,
22:       :cloudinary_video_url,
23:       :id,
24:       :path,
25:       :public_reactions_count,
26:       :readable_publish_date,
27:       :reading_time,
28:       :title,
29:       :user_id,
30:       :public_reaction_categories,
31:     )
32: 
33:     # return displayed_comments_count (excluding low score comments) if it was calculated earlier
34:     attribute :comments_count, (lambda do |article|
35:       article.displayed_comments_count? ? article.displayed_comments_count : article.comments_count
36:     end)
37:     attribute :video_duration_string, &:video_duration_in_minutes
38:     attribute :published_at_int, ->(article) { article.published_at.to_i }
39:     attribute :tag_list, ->(article) { article.cached_tag_list.to_s.split(", ") }
40:     attribute :flare_tag, ->(article, params) { params.dig(:tag_flares, article.id) }
41:     
42:     # Only include special title methods for status articles
43:     attribute :title_finalized_for_feed, if: proc { |article| article.type_of == "status" }
44:     attribute :title_for_metadata, if: proc { |article| article.type_of == "status" }
45: 
46:     attribute :user do |article|
47:       user = article.user
48: 
49:       {
50:         name: user.name,
51:         profile_image_90: user.profile_image_90,
52:         username: user.username
53:       }
54:     end
55: 
56:     attribute :organization, if: proc { |a| a.organization.present? } do |article|
57:       organization = article.organization
58:

61:         profile_image_90: article.organization.profile_image_90,

1: module Articles
2:   module Feeds
3:     class Basic
4:       def initialize(user: nil, number_of_articles: Article::DEFAULT_FEED_PAGINATION_WINDOW_SIZE, page: 1, tag: nil)
5:         @user = user
6:         @number_of_articles = number_of_articles
7:         @page = [page, 1].max
8:         @tag = tag
9:         @article_score_applicator = Articles::Feeds::ArticleScoreCalculatorForUser.new(user: @user)
10:       end
11: 
12:       def default_home_feed(**_kwargs)
13:         articles = Article.published
14:           .order(hotness_score: :desc)
15:           .with_at_least_home_feed_minimum_score
16:           .limit(@number_of_articles)
17:           .offset((@page - 1) * @number_of_articles)
18:           .limited_column_select
19:           .includes(:distinct_reaction_categories, :context_notes, top_comments: :user)
20:           .from_subforem
21: 
22:         return articles unless @user
23: 
24:         articles = articles.where.not(user_id: UserBlock.cached_blocked_ids_for_blocker(@user.id))
25:         if (hidden_tags = @user.cached_antifollowed_tag_names).any?
26:           articles = articles.not_cached_tagged_with_any(hidden_tags)
27:         end
28:         articles.sort_by.with_index do |article, index|
29:           tag_score = score_followed_tags(article)
30:           user_score = score_followed_user(article)
31:           org_score = score_followed_organization(article)
32: 
33:           # NOTE: Not quite understanding the purpose of the `-
34:           # index`.  My guess is that it helps reduce the impact of the
35:           # hotness score on the sort order.
36:           tag_score + org_score + user_score - index
37:         end.reverse!
38:       end
39: 
40:       private
41: 
42:       # Alias :feed to preserve past implementations, but favoring a
43:       # convergence of interface implementations.
44:       alias feed default_home_feed
45: 
46:       # Creating :more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized to conform
47:       # to the public interface of
48:       # Articles::Feeds::LargeForemExperimental
49:       alias more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized default_home_feed
50: 
51:       delegate(:score_followed_tags,
52:                :score_followed_user,
53:                :score_followed_organization,
54:                to: :@article_score_applicator)
55:     end
56:   end
57: end

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/forem/app/services/articles/feeds/large_forem_experimental.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: module Articles
2:   module Feeds
3:     class LargeForemExperimental
4:       def initialize(user: nil, number_of_articles: Article::DEFAULT_FEED_PAGINATION_WINDOW_SIZE, page: 1, tag: nil)
5:         @user = user
6:         @number_of_articles = number_of_articles
7:         @page = page
8:         @tag = tag
9:         @article_score_applicator = Articles::Feeds::ArticleScoreCalculatorForUser.new(user: user)
10:       end
11: 
12:       def default_home_feed(user_signed_in: false)
13:         _featured_story, stories = featured_story_and_default_home_feed(user_signed_in: user_signed_in, ranking: true)
14:         stories
15:       end
16: 
17:       # @param user_signed_in [Boolean] are we treating this as an
18:       #        anonymous user?
19:       # @param ranking [Boolean] if true, apply a ranking algorithm
20:       # @param must_have_main_image [Boolean] if true, the featured
21:       #        story must have a main image
22:       #
23:       # @note the must_have_main_image parameter name matches PR #15240
24:       def featured_story_and_default_home_feed(user_signed_in: false, ranking: true, must_have_main_image: true)
25:         featured_story, hot_stories = globally_hot_articles(user_signed_in, must_have_main_image: must_have_main_image)
26:         hot_stories = rank_and_sort_articles(hot_stories) if @user && ranking
27:         [featured_story, hot_stories]
28:       end
29: 
30:       # Adding an alias to preserve public method signature.
31:       # Eventually, we should be able to remove the alias.
32:       alias default_home_feed_and_featured_story featured_story_and_default_home_feed
33: 
34:       def more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized
35:         _featured_story, stories = featured_story_and_default_home_feed(user_signed_in: true)
36:         first_quarter(stories).shuffle + last_three_quarters(stories)
37:       end
38: 
39:       # Adding an alias to preserve public method signature.  However,
40:       # in this code base there are no further references of
41:       # :more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized_at_end
42:       alias more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized_at_end more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized
43: 
44:       # @api private
45:       def rank_and_sort_articles(articles)
46:         ranked_articles = articles.each_with_object({}) do |article, result|
47:           article_points = score_single_article(article)
48:           result[article] = article_points
49:         end
50:         ranked_articles = ranked_articles.sort_by { |_article, article_points| -article_points }.map(&:first)
51:         ranked_articles.to(@number_of_articles - 1)
52:       end
53: 
54:       # @api private
55:       def score_single_article(article, base_article_points: 0)
56:         article_points = base_article_points
57:         article_points += score_followed_user(article)
58:         article_points += score_followed_organization(article)
59:         article_points += score_followed_tags(article)
60:         article_points += score_experience_level(article)
61:         article_points += score_comments(article)
62:         article_points
63:       end
64: 
65:       delegate(:score_followed_user,
66:                :score_followed_tags,
67:                :score_followed_organization,
68:                :score_experience_level,
69:                :score_comments,
70:                to: :@article_score_applicator)
71: 
72:       # @api private
73:       # rubocop:disable Layout/LineLength
74:       def globally_hot_articles(user_signed_in, must_have_main_image: true, article_score_threshold: -15, min_rand_limit: 15, max_rand_limit: 80)
75:         # rubocop:enable Layout/LineLength
76:         if user_signed_in
77:           hot_stories = experimental_hot_story_grab
78:           hot_stories = hot_stories.not_authored_by(UserBlock.cached_blocked_ids_for_blocker(@user.id))
79:           featured_story = featured_story_from(stories: hot_stories, must_have_main_image: must_have_main_image)
80:           new_stories = Article.published.from_subforem
81:             .where("score > ?", article_score_threshold)
82:             .limited_column_select.includes(top_comments: :user)
83:             .order(published_at: :desc)
84:             .includes(:distinct_reaction_categories, :subforem, :context_notes)
85:             .limit(rand(min_rand_limit..max_rand_limit))
86:           hot_stories = hot_stories.to_a + new_stories.to_a
87:         else
88:           hot_stories = Article.published.from_subforem.limited_column_select
89:             .includes(:distinct_reaction_categories, :subforem, top_comments: :user)
90:             .page(@page).per(@number_of_articles)
91:             .with_at_least_home_feed_minimum_score
92:             .order(hotness_score: :desc)
93:           featured_story = featured_story_from(stories: hot_stories, must_have_main_image: must_have_main_image)
94:         end
95:         [featured_story, hot_stories.to_a]
96:       end
97: 
98:       private
99: 
100:       def featured_story_from(stories:, must_have_main_image:)
101:         return stories.first unless must_have_main_image
102: 
103:         stories.where.not(main_image: nil).first
104:       end
105: 
106:       def experimental_hot_story_grab
107:         start_time = Articles::Feeds.oldest_published_at_to_consider_for(user: @user)
108:         Article.published.limited_column_select.includes(top_comments: :user)
109:           .includes(:distinct_reaction_categories, :subforem, :context_notes)
110:           .where("published_at > ?", start_time)
111:           .page(@page).per(@number_of_articles)
112:           .order(score: :desc)
113:       end
114: 
115:       def first_quarter(array)
116:         array[0...(array.length / 4)]
117:       end
118: 
119:       def last_three_quarters(array)
120:         array[(array.length / 4)..array.length]
121:       end
122:     end
123:   end
124: end

1: module Articles
2:   module Feeds
3:     TIME_AGO_MAX = Rails.env.production? ? 10.days.ago : 90.days.ago
4: 
5:     class Custom
6:       def initialize(user: nil, number_of_articles: Article::DEFAULT_FEED_PAGINATION_WINDOW_SIZE, page: 1, tag: nil, feed_config: nil)
7:         @user = user
8:         @number_of_articles = number_of_articles
9:         @page = [page, 1].max
10:         @tag = tag
11:         @feed_config = feed_config
12:       end
13: 
14:       def default_home_feed(**_kwargs)
15:         return [] if @feed_config.nil? || @user.nil?
16:         
17:         execute_feed_query
18:       end
19: 
20:       private
21: 
22:       def execute_feed_query
23:         # Optimize lookback calculation - cache the result
24:         # Note: Partial indexes are optimized for 7-day lookback (covers 95%+ of queries)
25:         # If you change this, consider updating the partial indexes in the migration
26:         lookback_setting = Settings::UserExperience.feed_lookback_days.to_i
27:         lookback = lookback_setting.positive? ? lookback_setting.days.ago : TIME_AGO_MAX
28:         
29:         # Pre-calculate user-specific data to avoid repeated database calls
30:         user_data = preload_user_data
31:         
32:         # Build optimized base query with better index usage
33:         articles = build_optimized_base_query(lookback, user_data)
34:         
35:         # Apply user-specific filters early in the query
36:         articles = apply_user_filters(articles, user_data)
37:         
38:         # Apply subforem-specific filters
39:         articles = apply_subforem_filters(articles)
40:         
41:         # Apply weighted shuffle if needed
42:         articles = weighted_shuffle(articles, @feed_config.shuffle_weight) if @feed_config.shuffle_weight.positive?
43:         
44:         # Randomly shuffle top 5 articles if all articles are recent (within a week)
45:         articles = shuffle_top_five_if_recent(articles)
46:         
47:         articles
48:       end
49: 
50: 
51: 
52:       def preload_user_data
53:         {
54:           blocked_user_ids: UserBlock.cached_blocked_ids_for_blocker(@user.id),
55:           hidden_tags: @user.cached_antifollowed_tag_names,
56:           user_activity: @user.user_activity
57:         }
58:       end
59: 
60:       def build_optimized_base_query(lookback, user_data)
61:         # Use a more efficient query structure with better index hints
62:         base_query = Article.published
63:           .with_at_least_home_feed_minimum_score
64:           .where("articles.published_at > ?", lookback)
65:           .select("articles.*, (#{score_sql_method}) as computed_score")
66:           .order(Arel.sql("computed_score DESC"))
67:           .limit(@number_of_articles)
68:           .offset((@page - 1) * @number_of_articles)
69:           .limited_column_select
70:           .includes(:subforem) # Only include essential associations
71:           .from_subforem
72: 
73:         # Include necessary associations for performance
74:         base_query = base_query.includes(:distinct_reaction_categories, :context_notes, top_comments: :user)
75:         
76:         base_query
77:       end
78: 
79:       def score_sql_method
80:         @feed_config.score_sql(@user)
81:       end
82: 
83: 
84:       def apply_user_filters(articles, user_data)
85:         # Apply user-specific filters early to reduce dataset size
86:         if user_data[:blocked_user_ids].any?
87:           articles = articles.where.not(user_id: user_data[:blocked_user_ids])
88:         end
89:         
90:         if user_data[:hidden_tags].any?
91:           articles = if ENV["OPTIMIZED_FEED_TAGS_QUERY"] == "true"
92:                        articles.where.not("articles.tags_array && ARRAY[?]::text[]", user_data[:hidden_tags].map(&:to_s))
93:                      else
94:                        articles.not_cached_tagged_with_any(user_data[:hidden_tags])
95:                      end
96:         end
97:         
98:         articles
99:       end
100: 
101:       def apply_subforem_filters(articles)
102:         # Apply subforem-specific filters
103:         if RequestStore.store[:subforem_id] == RequestStore.store[:root_subforem_id]
104:           articles = articles.where(type_of: :full_post)
105:         end
106:         
107:         articles
108:       end
109: 
110:       def weighted_shuffle(arr, shuffle_weight)
111:         # Each element gets a new sort key: its original index plus a random offset.
112:         # We choose the random offset uniformly from -2*shuffle_weight to 2*shuffle_weight.
113:         # The average absolute offset then is (2*shuffle_weight)/2 = shuffle_weight.
114:         arr.each_with_index.sort_by do |item, index|
115:           index + (rand * (4 * shuffle_weight) - 2 * shuffle_weight)
116:         end.map(&:first)
117:       end
118: 
119:       def shuffle_top_five_if_recent(articles)
120:         return articles if articles.empty?
121:         
122:         # Check if all articles are published within the last week
123:         one_week_ago = 1.week.ago
124:         all_recent = articles.all? { |article| article.published_at > one_week_ago }
125:         
126:         return articles unless all_recent
127:         
128:         # Use dynamic shuffle count only if recent_page_views_shuffle_weight > 0
129:         shuffle_count = if @feed_config.recent_page_views_shuffle_weight > 0.0
130:                           calculate_dynamic_shuffle_count
131:                         else
132:                           5 # Default behavior
133:                         end
134:         
135:         # Split articles into top N (based on shuffle count) and the rest
136:         top_articles = articles.first(shuffle_count)
137:         rest = articles[shuffle_count..-1] || []
138:         
139:         # Randomly shuffle the top articles
140:         shuffled_top_articles = top_articles.shuffle
141:         
142:         # Return shuffled top articles + unshuffled rest
143:         shuffled_top_articles + rest
144:       end
145: 
146:       def calculate_dynamic_shuffle_count
147:         return 5 unless @user&.user_activity&.recently_viewed_articles&.any?
148:         
149:         # Get the most recent page view timestamp
150:         most_recent_page_view = @user.user_activity.recently_viewed_articles.first
151:         return 5 unless most_recent_page_view&.size >= 2
152:         
153:         begin
154:           most_recent_timestamp = most_recent_page_view[1].to_datetime
155:           hours_since_last_view = ((Time.current - most_recent_timestamp) / 1.hour).ceil
156:           
157:           # Calculate base shuffle count: 20 for within 1 hour, decreasing by 1 for each additional hour
158:           # Minimum of 5 (original behavior)
159:           base_shuffle_count = [21 - hours_since_last_view, 5].max
160:           
161:           # Apply the weight multiplier
162:           weighted_shuffle_count = (base_shuffle_count * @feed_config.recent_page_views_shuffle_weight).round
163:           
164:           # Ensure minimum of 5 and maximum of 20
165:           [[weighted_shuffle_count, 5].max, 20].min
166:         rescue StandardError
167:           # If there's any error parsing the timestamp, fall back to default
168:           5
169:         end
170:       end
171: 
172:       # Preserve the public interface
173:       alias feed default_home_feed
174:       alias more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized default_home_feed
175:     end
176:   end
177: end

(End of file - total 177 lines)
</content>

4:       # @param stories [ActiveRecord::Relation, #detect] pick a
5:       #        featured story from this enumerable
6:       # @param must_have_main_image [Boolean] if true, the featured
7:       #        story must have a main image
8:       # @return [Article]
9:       #
10:       # @note the must_have_main_image parameter name matches PR #15240
11:       #
12:       # @note One might assume that this would query on the
13:       #       `Articles.where(featured: true)` but in my sleuthing,
14:       #       the origin of this method's inner logic never included a
15:       #       consideration for `featured == true`.  Perhaps that
16:       #       should change?  This has been reported in
17:       #       https://github.com/forem/forem/issues/15613 and impacts
18:       #       Articles::Feeds::VariantQuery
19:       def self.call(stories, must_have_main_image: true)

21:           if must_have_main_image

24:             else
25:               stories.detect { |story| story.main_image.present? }
26:             end

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: module Articles
2:   module Feeds
3:     # This object is responsible for building the relevancy feed based on the given user and the
4:     # variant they are currently assigned.
5:     #
6:     # This class uses the Articles::Feeds::VariantQuery::Config to create a SQL statement.  The SQL
7:     # statement calculates a `relevancy_score` for each article based on the configuration of the
8:     # chosen relevancy levers.
9:     #
10:     # @see config/feed-variants/README.md
11:     # @see app/models/articles/feeds/README.md
12:     class VariantQuery
13:       Config = Struct.new(
14:         :variant,
15:         :description,
16:         :levers, # Array <Articles::Feeds::RelevancyLever::Configured>
17:         :order_by, # Articles::Feeds::OrderByLever
18:         :max_days_since_published,
19:         # when true, each time you call the query you will get different randomized numbers; when
20:         # false, the resulting randomized numbers will be the same within a window of time.
21:         :reseed_randomizer_on_each_request,
22:         keyword_init: true,
23:       ) do
24:         alias_method :reseed_randomizer_on_each_request?, :reseed_randomizer_on_each_request
25:       end
26:       # @api public
27:       #
28:       # @param variant [Symbol, #to_sym] the name of the variant query we're building.
29:       # @param assembler [Articles::Feeds::VariantAssembler, #call] responsible for converting the
30:       #        given variant to a config suitable for building a VariantQuery.
31:       # @param kwargs [Hash] named parameters to pass along to the #initialize method.
32:       #
33:       # @return [Articles::Feeds::VariantQuery]
34:       #
35:       # @see #initialize
36:       def self.build_for(variant:, assembler: VariantAssembler, **kwargs)
37:         config = assembler.call(variant: variant)
38:         new(config: config, **kwargs)
39:       end
40: 
41:       # @param config [Articles::Feeds::VariantQuery::Config]
42:       # @param user [User,NilClass]
43:       # @param number_of_articles [Integer, #to_i]
44:       # @param page [Integer, #to_i]
45:       # @param tag [NilClass] not used
46:       #
47:       # @param seed [Number] used in the `setseed` Postgresql function to set the randomization
48:       #        seed.  This parameter allows the caller (and debugger) to use the same randomization
49:       #        order in the queries; the hope being that this might help in any debugging.
50:       def initialize(config:, user: nil, number_of_articles: 50, page: 1, tag: nil, seed: nil, type_of: "discover")
51:         @user = user
52:         @number_of_articles = number_of_articles
53:         @page = page
54:         @tag = tag
55:         @config = config
56:         @type_of = type_of
57:         @seed = randomizer_seed_for(seed: seed, user: user)
58:         oldest_published_at = Articles::Feeds.oldest_published_at_to_consider_for(
59:           user: @user,
60:           days_since_published: config.max_days_since_published,
61:         )
62:         @query_parameters = {
63:           oldest_published_at: oldest_published_at,
64:           conditional_lookback: oldest_published_at - 12.hours,
65:           conditional_comment_timeframe: 6.hours.ago
66:         }
67:         configure!
68:       end
69: 
70:       attr_reader :config, :query_parameters, :seed
71: 
72:       # Query for articles relevant to the user's interest.
73:       #
74:       # @param only_featured [Boolean] select only articles that are "featured"
75:       # @param must_have_main_image [Boolean] select only articles that have a main image.
76:       # @param limit [Integer] the number of records to return
77:       # @param offset [Integer] start the paging window at the given offset
78:       # @param omit_article_ids [Array] don't include these articles in the search results
79:       #
80:       # @return ActiveRecord::Relation for Article
81:       #
82:       # @note This creates a complicated SQL query; well actually an ActiveRecord::Relation object
83:       #       on which you can call `to_sql`.  Which you might find helpful to see what's really
84:       #       going on.  A great place to do this is in the corresponding spec file.  See the
85:       #       example below:
86:       #
87:       # @example
88:       #
89:       #    user = User.first
90:       #    strategy = Articles::Feed::VariantQuery.build_for(variant: :original, user: user)
91:       #    puts strategy.call.to_sql
92:       #
93:       # rubocop:disable Layout/LineLength
94:       def call(only_featured: false, must_have_main_image: false, limit: default_limit, offset: default_offset, omit_article_ids: [], comments_variant: default_comments_variant)
95:         # rubocop:enable Layout/LineLength
96: 
97:         # These are the variables we'll pass to the SQL statement.
98:         repeated_query_variables = query_parameters.merge(
99:           omit_article_ids: omit_article_ids,
100:           now: Time.current,
101:           user_id: @user&.id,
102:         )
103: 
104:         # This needs to be an Array for Article.sanitize_sql.
105:         unsanitized_sql_sub_query = [
106:           sql_sub_query(
107:             only_featured: only_featured,
108:             must_have_main_image: must_have_main_image,
109:             limit: limit,
110:             offset: offset,
111:             omit_article_ids: omit_article_ids,
112:           ),
113:           repeated_query_variables,
114:         ]
115: 
116:         # Following this blog post: https://pganalyze.com/blog/active-record-subqueries-rails#the-from-subquery
117:         #
118:         # We're using the above `unsanitized_sql_sub_query` to create a result set so we can join to
119:         # articles and then sort on the attributes on either the article or the result set
120:         # (e.g. sort on the relevancy score).
121:         join_fragment = Arel.sql(
122:           "INNER JOIN (" \
123:           "\n--- The setseed needs to be called independently; later we reference seeder \n" \
124:           "WITH seeder AS (SELECT setseed(#{Float(@seed)})) " \
125:           "\n--- We are using the inner logic to build relevancy score" \
126:           "\n--- The outer part, with seeder, is to create a stable randomized number \n" \
127:           "SELECT inner_article_relevancies.id, " \
128:           "inner_article_relevancies.relevancy_score, " \
129:           "RANDOM() AS randomized_value " \
130:           "FROM seeder, " \
131:           "(#{Article.sanitize_sql(unsanitized_sql_sub_query)}) AS inner_article_relevancies" \
132:           ") AS article_relevancies ON articles.id = article_relevancies.id",
133:         )
134: 
135:         # This sub-query allows us to take the hard work of the hand-coded unsanitized sql and
136:         # create a sub-query that we can use to help ensure that we can use all of the ActiveRecord
137:         # goodness of scopes (e.g., limited_column_select) and eager includes.
138:         scope = Article.joins(join_fragment)
139:           .from_subforem
140:           .minimal_feed_column_select
141:           .includes(:distinct_reaction_categories, :context_notes)
142:           .includes(:subforem)
143:           .order(config.order_by.to_sql)
144: 
145:         scope = case comments_variant
146:                 when "top_comments"
147:                   scope.includes(top_comments: :user)
148:                 when "more_inclusive_top_comments"
149:                   scope.includes(more_inclusive_top_comments: :user)
150:                 when "recent_good_comments"
151:                   scope.includes(recent_good_comments: :user)
152:                 when "more_inclusive_recent_good_comments"
153:                   scope.includes(more_inclusive_recent_good_comments: :user)
154:                 when "most_inclusive_recent_good_comments"
155:                   scope.includes(most_inclusive_recent_good_comments: :user)
156:                 else
157:                   scope.includes(top_comments: :user) # fallback default
158:                 end
159: 
160:         if @user.present? && (hidden_tags = @user.cached_antifollowed_tag_names).any?
161:           scope = scope.not_cached_tagged_with_any(hidden_tags)
162:         end
163: 
164:         scope
165:       end
166: 
167:       alias more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized call
168: 
169:       # Provided as a means to align interfaces with existing feeds.
170:       #
171:       # @note I really dislike this method name as it is opaque on
172:       #       it's purpose.
173:       # @note We're specifically In the LargeForemExperimental implementation, the
174:       #       default home feed omits the featured story.  In this
175:       #       case, I don't want to do that.  Instead, I want to see
176:       #       how this behaves.
177:       def default_home_feed(**)
178:         call
179:       end
180: 
181:       # The featured story should be the article that:
182:       #
183:       # - has the highest relevance score for the nil_user version
184:       # - has a main image (see note below).
185:       #
186:       # The other articles should use the nil_user version and require the `featured = true`
187:       # attribute.  In my envisioned implementation, the pagination would omit the featured story.
188:       #
189:       # @return [Array<Article, Array<Article>] a featured story
190:       #         Article and an array of Article objects.
191:       #
192:       # @note Per prior work, a featured story is the article that has a main image, is marked as
193:       #       featured (e.g., featured = true), and has the highest relevance score.  In the
194:       #       Articles::Feeds::LargeForemExperimental object we used the hotness_score to determine
195:       #       which to use.  The hotness score is most analogue to how this class calculates the
196:       #       relevance score when we don't have a user.
197:       #
198:       # @note There are requests to allow for the featured article to NOT require a main image.
199:       #       We're still talking through what that means.  This work relates to PR #15333.
200:       #
201:       # @note including the ** operator to mirror the method interface of the other feed strategies.
202:       #
203:       # @todo In other implementations, when user's aren't signed in we favor featured stories.  But
204:       #       not so much that they're in the featured story.  For non-signed in users, we may want
205:       #       to use a completely different set of scoring methods.
206:       #
207:       # @note The logic of Articles::Feeds::FindFeaturedStory does not (at present) filter apply an
208:       #       `Article.featured` scope.  [@jeremyf] I have reported this in
209:       #       https://github.com/forem/forem/issues/15613 to get clarity from product.
210:       def featured_story_and_default_home_feed(**)
211:         # the below implementation creates additional downstream complexities.
212:         articles = call
213:         featured_story = Articles::Feeds::FindFeaturedStory.call(articles)
214:         [featured_story, articles]
215:       end
216: 
217:       private
218: 
219:       def configure!
220:         @relevance_score_components = []
221: 
222:         # By default we always need to group by the `articles.id` column.  And as we apply relevancy
223:         # levers to the query, we need to add additional group_by clauses based on the chosen
224:         # relevancy levers.
225:         @group_by_fields = Set.new
226:         @group_by_fields << "articles.id"
227: 
228:         @joins = Set.new
229: 
230:         # Ensure that we honor a user's block requests.
231:         unless @user.nil?
232:           @joins << "LEFT OUTER JOIN user_blocks
233:             ON user_blocks.blocked_id = articles.user_id
234:               AND user_blocks.blocked_id IS NULL
235:               AND user_blocks.blocker_id = :user_id"
236:         end
237: 
238:         config.levers.each do |lever|
239:           # Don't attempt to use this factor if we don't have user.
240:           next if lever.user_required? && @user.nil?
241: 
242:           # This scoring method requires a group by clause.
243:           @group_by_fields << lever.group_by_fragment if lever.group_by_fragment.present?
244: 
245:           @joins += lever.joins_fragments if lever.joins_fragments.present?
246: 
247:           @relevance_score_components << build_score_element_from(
248:             select_fragment: lever.select_fragment,
249:             cases: lever.cases,
250:             fallback: lever.fallback,
251:           )
252: 
253:           # As implemented, this is not looking for collisions of named parameters.
254:           @query_parameters.merge!(lever.query_parameters)
255:         end
256:       end
257: 
258:       # Concatenate the required group by clauses.
259:       #
260:       # @return [String]
261:       def group_by_fields_as_sql
262:         @group_by_fields.join(", ")
263:       end
264: 
265:       # The sql statement for selecting based on relevance scores
266:       def sql_sub_query(limit:, offset:, omit_article_ids:, only_featured: false, must_have_main_image: false)
267:         where_clause = build_sql_with_where_clauses(
268:           only_featured: only_featured,
269:           must_have_main_image: must_have_main_image,
270:           omit_article_ids: omit_article_ids,
271:         )
272:         <<~THE_SQL_STATEMENT
273:           SELECT articles.id, (#{relevance_score_components_as_sql}) as relevancy_score
274:           FROM articles
275:           #{joins_clauses_as_sql}
276:           WHERE #{where_clause}
277:           GROUP BY #{group_by_fields_as_sql}
278:           ORDER BY relevancy_score DESC,
279:             articles.published_at DESC
280:           #{offset_and_limit_clause(offset: offset, limit: limit)}
281:         THE_SQL_STATEMENT
282:       end
283: 
284:       def build_sql_with_where_clauses(only_featured:, must_have_main_image:, omit_article_ids:)
285:         # Hardcode the values for lookback_hours and comment_hours.
286:         where_clauses = "articles.published = true"
287:         where_clauses += " AND articles.published_at < :now"
288:         where_clauses += " AND articles.score >= 0"
289:         if @type_of == "discover"
290:           where_clauses += " AND ((articles.published_at > :oldest_published_at)
291:             OR (articles.published_at > :conditional_lookback
292:             AND articles.last_comment_at > :conditional_comment_timeframe))"
293:         elsif @user
294:           user_ids = @user.cached_following_users_ids + @user.cached_following_organizations_ids + [0] # Adding one that will never be reached so we can have a valid SQL statement
295:           where_clauses += " AND articles.user_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')})"
296:         end
297:         where_clauses += " AND articles.id NOT IN (:omit_article_ids)" unless omit_article_ids.compact.empty?
298:         where_clauses += " AND articles.featured = true" if only_featured

308:         end

313:         @joins.join("\n")

319:       end

327: 
328:         (@page.to_i - 1) * default_limit

333:       end

335:       # We want to ensure that we're not randomizing someone's feed all the time; and instead aiming
336:       # for somewhat repeatable experiences (e.g. I refresh the page it is likely I will have the
337:       # same order of pages even though we've randomized things a bit).
338:       def randomizer_seed_for(seed:, user:)
339:         return Float(seed) if seed
340: 
341:         return rand if config.reseed_randomizer_on_each_request?
342: 
343:         # This is added as a short-circuit in-case the caching proves to be non-performant.  Once
344:         # this has been merged, given that it's part of the main loop, we can remove the FeatureFlag
345:         # a week or so after we merge.
346:         return rand if FeatureFlag.enabled?(:halt_caching_for_feeds_random_seed)
347: 
348:         # From https://api.rubyonrails.org/classes/ActiveSupport/Cache/Store.html#method-i-fetch
349:         #
350:         # Setting :race_condition_ttl is very useful in situations where a cache entry is used very
351:         # frequently and is under heavy load. If a cache expires and due to heavy load several
352:         # different processes will try to read data natively and then they all will try to write to
353:         # cache. To avoid that case the first process to find an expired cache entry will bump the
354:         # cache expiration time by the value set in :race_condition_ttl.
355:         Rails.cache.fetch(
356:           "variant_query-for-#{user&.id || 'no-one'}",
357:           race_condition_ttl: 10.seconds,
358:           expires_in: 10.minutes,
359:         ) { rand }
360:       end

362:       # Responsible for transforming the :select_fragment, :cases, and :fallback into a SQL fragment
363:       # that we can use to multiply with the other SQL fragments.
364:       #
365:       # @param select_fragment [String]
366:       # @param cases [Array<Array<#to_i, #to_f>>]
367:       # @param fallback [#to_f]
368:       def build_score_element_from(select_fragment:, cases:, fallback:)
369:         values = []
370:         # I would love to sanitize this, but alas, we must trust this clause.
371:         text = "(CASE #{select_fragment}"
372:         cases.each do |value, factor|
373:           text += "\nWHEN ? THEN ?"
374:           values << value.to_i
375:           values << factor.to_f
376:         end
377:         text += "\nELSE ? END)"
378:         values << fallback.to_f
379:         values.unshift(text)
380: 
381:         Article.sanitize_sql_array(values)

1: class ApplicationRecord < ActiveRecord::Base
2:   self.abstract_class = true
3: 
4:   include Purgeable
5: 
6:   # see <https://www.postgresql.org/docs/11/catalog-pg-class.html> for details
7:   # on the `pg_class` table
8:   QUERY_ESTIMATED_COUNT = <<~SQL.squish.freeze
9:     SELECT (
10:       (reltuples / GREATEST(relpages, 1)) *
11:       (pg_relation_size(?) / (GREATEST(current_setting('block_size')::integer, 1)))
12:     )::bigint AS count
13:     FROM pg_class
14:     WHERE relname = ? AND relkind = 'r';
15:   SQL
16: 
17:   # Computes an estimated count of the number of rows using stats collected by VACUUM
18:   # inspired by <https://www.citusdata.com/blog/2016/10/12/count-performance/#dup_counts_estimated_full>
19:   # and <https://stackoverflow.com/a/48391562/4186181>
20:   def self.estimated_count
21:     query = sanitize_sql_array([QUERY_ESTIMATED_COUNT, table_name, table_name])
22:     result = connection.execute(query)
23: 
24:     count = result.first["count"]
25:     result.clear # PG::Result is manually managed in memory, we need to release its resources
26:     count
27:   end
28: 
29:   # Decorate collection with appropriate decorator
30:   def self.decorate
31:     decorator_class.decorate_collection(all)
32:   end
33: 
34:   # Infers the decorator class to be used by (e.g. `User` maps to `UserDecorator`).
35:   # adapted from https://github.com/drapergem/draper/blob/157eb955072a941e6455e0121fca09a989fcbc21/lib/draper/decoratable.rb#L71
36:   def self.decorator_class(called_on = self)
37:     prefix = respond_to?(:model_name) ? model_name : name
38:     decorator_name = "#{prefix}Decorator"
39:     decorator_name_constant = decorator_name.safe_constantize
40:     return decorator_name_constant unless decorator_name_constant.nil?
41: 
42:     return superclass.decorator_class(called_on) if superclass.respond_to?(:decorator_class)
43: 
44:     raise UninferrableDecoratorError,
45:           I18n.t("models.application_record.uninferrable", class: called_on.class.name)
46:   end
47: 
48:   def self.statement_timeout
49:     connection.execute("SELECT setting FROM pg_settings WHERE name = 'statement_timeout'")
50:       .first["setting"]
51:       .to_i
52:       .seconds / 1000
53:   end
54: 
55:   def self.with_statement_timeout(duration, connection: self.connection)
56:     original_timeout = statement_timeout
57:     milliseconds = (duration.to_f * 1000).to_i
58:     connection.execute "SET statement_timeout = #{milliseconds}"
59:     yield
60:   ensure
61:     milliseconds = original_timeout.to_i * 1000
62:     connection.execute "SET statement_timeout = #{milliseconds}"
63:   end
64: 
65:   # Used for high-volume inserts to prevent unnecessary locking and blocking of other operations
66:   def self.with_synchronous_commit_off
67:     transaction do
68:       connection.execute("SET LOCAL synchronous_commit TO OFF")
69:       yield
70:     end
71:   end
72: 
73:   # ActiveRecord's `find_each` method allows you to work with a large collection of records
74:   # in batches, but strictly only orders those batches by IDs in ascending order.
75:   # Any other specified order is either ignored or raises an error (depending on configuration).
76:   # This method allows performing batch queries of arbitrary order.
77:   #
78:   # @param batch_size [Integer] Batch size limit
79:   # @yieldparam [self]
80:   # @return [Enumerator<self>] if no block is given
81:   #
82:   # @see https://api.rubyonrails.org/v7.0.4.2/classes/ActiveRecord/Batches.html#method-i-find_each
83:   def self.find_each_respecting_scope(batch_size: 1000, &block)
84:     load_in_batches = Enumerator.new do |e|
85:       in_batches_respecting_scope(batch_size: batch_size) do |batch|
86:         batch.each { |record| e.yield record }
87:       end
88:     end
89: 
90:     return load_in_batches unless block
91: 
92:     load_in_batches.each(&block)
93:   end
94: 
95:   def self.in_batches_respecting_scope(batch_size: 1000)
96:     relation = self
97:

# Article contract audit (Forem)

## Step 1: Orient

This is the **Forem** Rails monorepo (powers dev.to). Code lives in `app/`, `lib/`, `db/`, `spec/`. The central content model is `app/models/article.rb`. Article-specific logic is clustered in:

- Model + shared concerns: `app/models/article.rb`, `app/models/concerns/`, `app/models/articles/`
- Lifecycle services: `app/services/articles/`
- Background workers: `app/workers/articles/` + notification/reaction/concept workers
- Feeds/ranking: `app/services/articles/feeds/`, `app/models/articles/feeds.rb`
- Search: `app/models/concerns/algolia_searchable/searchable_article.rb`, `app/services/search/article.rb`, `app/queries/homepage/articles_query.rb`
- Caching: `app/services/edge_cache/`, `app/models/articles/cached_entity.rb`

## Step 5: Identity and visibility guards

- **Controllers/API** generally use `Article.find`, `Article.find_by`, or `Article.published.from_subforem` scopes before acting.
- **`Articles::Updater`** checks `became_unpublished?`/`remains_published?`/`became_published?` at `app/services/articles/updater.rb:64-74`.
- **`Notification.send_to_mentioned_users_and_followers`** returns unless `notifiable.is_a?(Article) && notifiable.published? && notifiable.type_of == "full_post"` at `app/models/notification.rb:65`.
- **`Notifications::NotifiableAction::Send`** returns unless `notifiable.is_a?(Article)` at `app/services/notifications/notifiable_action/send.rb:20`.
- **`Mentions::CreateAll`** handles the `Article` case inline at `app/services/mentions/create_all.rb:85`.
- **`Articles::ScoreCalcWorker`** returns unless `Article.find_by(id: ...)` exists (`app/workers/articles/score_calc_worker.rb:8-9`).
- **`Articles::GenerateSummaryWorker`** returns unless article exists, then guards on score ≥ 50 / comment_score ≥ 25 (`app/workers/articles/generate_summary_worker.rb:13-16`).
- **`Articles::GenerateFreeformContextNoteWorker`** guards on score, published_at age, and existing context notes (`app/workers/articles/generate_freeform_context_note_worker.rb:9-15`).
- **`GenerateArticleEmbeddingWorker`** guards on `article.published?` and `respond_to?(:semantic_embedding)` (`app/workers/generate_article_embedding_worker.rb:7`).
- **`Articles::UpdatePageViewsWorker`** guards on `article&.published?` and skips author views (`app/workers/articles/update_page_views_worker.rb:37-39`).
- **`Articles::PageViewUpdater`** service guards on `Article.published.exists?` (`app/services/articles/page_view_updater.rb:20`).
- **`Articles::PublishWorker`** selects only `published: true` articles missing `context_notifications_published` (`app/workers/articles/publish_worker.rb:9-10`).
- **`Articles::QualityReactionWorker`** uses `Article.published` scope with score/type filters (`app/workers/articles/quality_reaction_worker.rb:20-28`).
- **`EdgeCache::BustArticle`** returns unless article and checks `article.published`/`published_at`/`hotness_score` rankings (`app/services/edge_cache/bust_article.rb:9-44`).
- **`EdgeCache::BustComment`** checks `commentable.is_a?(Article)` (`app/services/edge_cache/bust_comment.rb:11`).
- **`Articles::UpdateArticleActivityWorker`** verifies `Article.exists?(id:)` before creating activity (`app/workers/articles/update_article_activity_worker.rb:89`).
- **`Comments::CalculateScore`** checks `comment.commentable_type == "Article"` and `commentable.is_a?(Article)` (`app/services/comments/calculate_score.rb:22/30`).
- **`Reaction`** callbacks guard with `reactable_type == "Article"` and `reactable.is_a?(Article)` (`app/models/reaction.rb:257/279/292`).

## Step 6: Blast-radius assessment (ranked)

**Critical / most likely to break**
1. `app/models/article.rb:168-205` — the `dependent:` teardown declarations. Changing how attached data is destroyed/cleaned up directly affects comments (nullify), notifications, mentions, page views, polls, profile pins, rating votes, context notes, trend/concept memberships, etc.
2. `app/services/articles/destroyer.rb:5-23` — relies on `article.comments.ids` being stable before `destroy!`, then manually removes notifications. Any change to callback ordering or dependent deletion will leave orphaned notifications or N+1 cleanup issues.
3. `app/services/articles/updater.rb:64-97` — inline unpublish cleanup of notifications, mentions, and comment notifications. If Article teardown changes, these may delete too much/little.
4. `app/services/users/delete_articles.rb:8` — builds virtual `Article.new(article.attributes)` before destroying user articles. If the Article constructor or required attributes change, this raises.
5. `app/services/organizations/delete.rb:37` — bulk `update_all(cached_organization: nil)` on articles. Changing cached entity contract breaks this.
6. `app/models/notification.rb:64-78` and notification workers — assume Article type and existence; if articles can be deleted while workers are in flight, notifications may reference missing records.

**High risk**
7. `app/models/reaction.rb` — `counter_culture` columns on Article (`public_reactions_count`, `reactions_count`) and reaction callbacks that touch article caches/scores.
8. `app/models/comment.rb` — `dependent: :nullify` on Article means comments keep `commentable_type = "Article"` but lose `commentable_id` on destroy. Any teardown rework must consider orphaned comment queries.
9. `app/models/concerns/webpage_trackable.rb` / `liquid_embeddable.rb` — polymorphic references and after_destroy domain/ embed updates assume article still exists or was the record type.
10. `app/services/edge_cache/bust_article.rb` / `bust_comment.rb` — assume article path, published state, and hotness ranking remain valid.
11. `app/models/concerns/algolia_searchable/searchable_article.rb:42` — `indexable` depends on `published && score.positive? && published_at < Time.current`; teardown changes to score/published must keep this consistent.
12. `app/workers/articles/update_dependent_embeds_worker.rb` — reprocesses markdown for any record referencing this Article. If Article teardown removes content early, embeds will 404.
13. `app/services/mentions/create_all.rb` / `app/models/mention.rb` — depends on `notifiable.mentions`, `processed_html`, and Article mention notifications.

**Medium risk**
14. `app/services/articles/feeds/*` and `app/queries/homepage/articles_query.rb` — rely on `Article.published`, `from_subforem`, score, and `cached_tag_list`.
15. `app/services/analytics_service.rb` / `app/models/article_activity.rb` — aggregate counters keyed to article_id; teardown may require cache invalidation.
16. AI workers (`GenerateArticleEmbeddingWorker`, `GenerateSummaryWorker`, etc.) — guard on published/score; if those semantics change, embedding/summary jobs may silently skip.
17. `app/models/articles/cached_entity.rb` and `Organization`/`User` callbacks — cached entity serialization may drift.
